results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need for ...
results.content:
We present EurekAgent, an agent system for autonomous scientific discovery that coordinates off-the-shelf CLI agents through four environment engineering dimensions: (1) permissions engineering, to expose useful capabilities and resources while preventing research-integrity violations; (2) artifact engineering, to structure solutions, logs, and evaluation results as shared progress memory; (3) budget engineering, to enable budget-aware exploration with runtime and compute boundaries; and (4) human-in-the-loop engineering, to support easy human supervision and intervention.
Within this environment, the agent remains free to select its own research workflows and strategies. [...] We presented EurekAgent, an environment-engineered system for autonomous scientific discovery on metric-driven research tasks. Rather than prescribing detailed research
workflows, EurekAgent coordinates off-the-shelf CLI-agent sessions through a simple prepare-propose-implement loop, while shaping the surrounding environment for reliable
evaluation, shared progress memory, resource boundaries, and human oversight.
Using Claude Code as the CLI agent and GLM-5.1 as the base LLM, EurekAgent achieves new state-of-the-art results on all evaluated mathematics and kernel engineering tasks, and
ranks first on our evaluated MLE-Bench Lite subset. These results suggest that, as general-purpose CLI agents become more capable, carefully engineered scientific-discovery [...] We frame this
as environment engineering: building environments that amplify productive behaviors, such as open-ended exploration, systematic artifact management, and inter-agent collaboration, while suppressing harmful behaviors, such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight.
We present EurekAgent, an environment-engineered agent system for
metric-driven autonomous scientific discovery.
EurekAgent engineers the environment along four dimensions: permissions engineering for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; artifact engineering for filesystem and Git-based collaboration; budget engineering for budget-aware exploration; and human-in-the-loop engineering for easy human supervision and intervention.
EurekAgent sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple

results.title: [Literature Review] EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is ...
results.content:
The paper introduces EUREKAGENT, an environment-engineered agent system designed for autonomous scientific discovery in metric-driven research tasks. It posits that as large language models (LLMs) improve, the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery shifts from prescribing agent workflows to designing the environments in which agents operate—a concept termed "environment engineering." This involves constructing environments that foster productive behaviors like open-ended exploration, systematic artifact management, and inter-agent collaboration, while mitigating detrimental behaviors such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight. [...] EUREKAGENT coordinates off-the-shelf Command Line Interface (CLI) agents through a three-stage iterative loop:
$\text{PREPARE} \rightarrow {\text{PROPOSE}r \rightarrow {\text{IMPLEMENT}{r,p}}{p=1}^{P}}{r=1}^{R}$
Here, $R$ represents the maximum number of iteration rounds, and $P$ denotes the maximum number of parallel implementation sessions per round.

EUREKAGENT's environment engineering is structured along four dimensions:

`time-checking helper API`

EUREKAGENT, using Claude Code as the CLI agent and GLM-5.1 as the base LLM, achieved new state-of-the-art (SOTA) results across various domains: [...] Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo

# [Literature Review] EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery

results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For ...
results.content:
John: To wrap up, EUREKAGENT provides strong evidence for a paradigm shift. As large language models become more powerful general problem-solvers, the critical challenge for autonomous science may not be inventing ever-more-complex agent architectures. Instead, it's about designing the operational context that channels their capabilities toward reliable, reproducible, and trustworthy discovery. The key takeaway is this: it's not about building a smarter scientist; it's about building a better lab for the scientist you already have.

John: Thanks for listening. If you have any further questions, ask our AI assistant or drop a comment. [...] John: It's about creating what the paper, referencing Gibson's theory, calls 'affordances'. The environment is designed to make the 'right' actions easy and the 'wrong' actions impossible. Instead of telling the agent how to do research, you create a world where good research practices are the only viable path. EUREKAGENT's main contribution is demonstrating this philosophy through a system that coordinates off-the-shelf agents. It aims to achieve state-of-the-art results not by creating a new agent architecture, but by meticulously crafting the resources, constraints, and interfaces within which an existing agent operates. This shifts the research focus from agent logic to environmental design.

Noah: So what does this 'engineered environment' actually look like in practice? [...] Transcript

John: Welcome to Advanced AI Systems. Today's lecture is on 'EUREKAGENT: AGENT ENVIRONMENT ENGINEERING IS ALL YOU NEED FOR AUTONOMOUS SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERY'. We've seen a lot of recent work like 'ShinkaEvolve' and 'AlphaResearch' focusing on complex evolutionary systems or prescribed workflows for AI agents. This paper, from researchers at Tsinghua University, challenges that trend. It proposes that as models become more capable, the primary focus should shift from designing the agent itself to engineering the environment it operates in.

John: Yes, Noah?

Noah: Excuse me, Professor. So, to put it simply, instead of trying to build a smarter AI scientist from scratch, they're focused on building a better, safer laboratory for a general-purpose AI to work in?

results.title: LiveAgent Review: Features, Pricing, Pros & Cons 2026
results.content:
### Workflows and automation

LiveAgent offers a solid set of CS automation tools to streamline support. Automation rules can assign tickets, add tags, or send notifications, while time rules handle escalations and follow-ups.

On top of that, built-in SLAs let managers track response standards, and the call center includes IVR flows as well as queue management for voice routing. Also, there is the new AI answer assistant in the background that drafts responses in different tones and provides editing options to speed up agent productivity. [...] Here’s a closer look:

### Pros

 True omnichannel service experience: LiveAgent brings email tickets, chat, calls, and social messages into one unified inbox.
 Built-in AI tools on every plan: from Small to Enterprise, you get access to both an AI chatbot and an AI answer assistant.
 Multilingual by design: the UI and help center support multiple languages, making it suitable for global teams.
 Full call center included: starting with the Medium plan, you get voice, IVR, agent ratings, and SLA rules.

### Cons

 Feature complexity can overwhelm: some users mention it feels daunting at first due to the sheer number of options.
 Mobile app experience lags: feedback points to limited functionality and usability issues in the mobile interface. [...] Learn more about AI agents

### Live Chat (widget)

LiveAgent’s live chat tool is designed to be fast and responsive, with widgets that can be customized to match your brand. Agents can see real-time visitor tracking data and use typing previews to anticipate customer messages. In addition, they can send or receive file attachments during conversations.

The chat system also supports proactive invitations, so teams can reach out to visitors browsing specific pages or hesitating at checkout. Moreover, the widget is mobile-friendly, ensuring conversations work smoothly across devices.

Empower your field technicians to deliver smarter, faster service with Youreka—the leader in AI-driven data collection for field operations. As a Salesforce-native solution, Youreka ensures accurate, structured data capture online or offline, fueling Agentforce and enabling AI-powered automation and decision-making. Our intelligent mobile forms guide technicians through complex procedures, ensuring every critical data point is captured and seamlessly integrated into Salesforce Data Cloud. This creates a unified source of truth, optimizing operations and enhancing predictive maintenance, service efficiency, and customer satisfaction.

### Built for Complex Field Operations

Standardize every job with step-by-step instructions and dynamic logic. [...] ##### Offline Data Capture

Work seamlessly in low-connectivity environments with full offline functionality.

Quickly adapt forms and workflows without developer support.

##### AI-Powered Insights

Turn field data into summaries, recommendations, and actionable intelligence.

##### Salesforce Native

All data lives securely within Salesforce, no syncing or duplication required.
